text:
Once upon a time in a small, vibrant town surrounded by rolling hills and lush forests, lived a curious ten-year-old named Lily. She was known for her adventurous spirit and had climbed every tree in the backyard. One sunny afternoon, Lily decided she wanted to explore the Forbidden Forest. The name was a bit misleading; it was the only forest around. It contained all sorts of mystical creatures but had no forbidden knowledge—not that Lily minded.

 Armed with nothing but her courage and a map she’d borrowed from her grandma, who’d told her tales from there, Lily approached the forest. She wondered how all those creatures lived there. The owls hooted gracefully; the squirrels chattered away happily. But one creature stood out—Glimmerfoot, the fox. She was small but smart, and she beckoned Lily over.

“Come!” Glimmerfoot said. “There’s a clearing. It’s magical here.”

Lily followed the fox down through dappled sunlight
